Astrology evolved as a reliable predictive science based on the realization that celestial objects strongly influence people's lives. Studying these heavenly bodies can reveal a lot about human beings, their characteristics, fortunes, and future. To understand and perfect this science, it took India's yogis and Siddhas years of meticulous observation of the positioning and movements of the cosmic entities like the stars and planets and linking them with the events of the world and the people's fortunes. Thus evolved the highly dependable and widely followed forecasting system of astrology.
Astrology operates on the principle that the positioning of the planets at the time of birth of a person virtually decides the course of their lives and fortunes. Hence, these planets were plotted accordingly in a uniquely designed chart known as the natal chart, birth chart, or
. The natural zodiac comprises 12 signs, known as the zodiac signs, and is represented by the 12 sections of the horoscope. The planets as found placed in the sky at the birth time are plotted in these sections, known as the Houses, and it is this horoscope, the replica of the celestial map at the time of birth, which is analyzed to make predictions about the person concerned.
According to the ancient Vedic Astrology system, there are nine planets, the Grahas, which include the self-luminary Sun, around which all the planets rotate but exclude our mother earth. These nine planets, which hold such strong sway over the people's lives, are much revered as Navagrahas. These are Surya, the Sun; Chandra, the Moon; Mangala or Chevvai, Mars; Budha, Mercury; Brihaspati or Guru, Jupiter; Shukra, Venus; Shani, Saturn, Rahu, and Ketu.
Unlike other planets, Rahu and Ketu do not have physical existence and hence are known as Chaya Graha, the shadow planets. They only represent the points of intersection of the orbits of the Sun and the Moon, where Rahu denotes the Northern node of the Moon and Ketu, the Southern node.
Some of these nine planets are regarded as auspicious or benefic, while others are inauspicious or malefic. While Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, and the waxing Moon are held auspicious, the Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, and the waning Moon are considered otherwise.
The 12 horoscopic Houses, too, have their significance. The first House, called the Lagna or the Ascendant, is perhaps the most crucial and represents an individual's personality and physical features. Similarly, each of the other Houses, too, stands for a significant aspect of life.
Which planet is present in which House? Which planet is in conjunction with which other planet and where? Which planet casts its aspect on what House? What are the qualities of the planets placed in the various Houses? And what areas of life do those Houses represent? – All these form part of the horoscope reading or analysis and forecasting about a person and their future.

8. Can Astrology Predict About Lottery Winning?
Astrology can indicate periods of financial opportunity and luck but cannot predict specific events like winning the lottery. Lottery wins are highly random and not determined by
While astrologers can advise on auspicious times for financial ventures, relying on astrology for lottery predictions is not practical. It's best to view astrology as a tool for personal growth and informed decision-making rather than for predicting random chance events.

--- PAGE: Murugan | Murugan Temple | AstroVed Temple (https://www.astroved.com/archetype-deity-pooja-muruga-pooja--p104.aspx) ---

Murugan | Murugan Temple | AstroVed Temple
Muruga is the Divine warrior and son of Shiva and Parvati. He is a unique God and can help you eliminate obstacles and fulfill your desires. He is a very popular God in South India who is sought after for his blessings to destroy evil forces, resolve real estate problems, confer good health, and overcome debts. He is also called Skanda, Karthikeya, Saravana, and Subrahmanya. As per Vedic Astrology, Muruga is the overlord of the planet Mars. This fiery planet can cause much disruption to life, and hence propitiating Muruga, can help minimize the adverse effects of Mars.
: Please remember to choose your service start date after making the payment.
Muruga Pooja is beneficial to anyone looking for success in their undertakings, especially in real estate, and those who need to overcome debts and negative influences, and subdue an agitated mind. According to scriptures, performing this Pooja can help you attain Muruga’s grace to gain strength, vitality, and protection from enemies, and lead a peaceful life.
Water Abishekam (Hydration Ceremony) to Muruga
According to Sacred texts, performing Water Abishekam with devotion and faith can invoke Muruga's blessings to wash away impurities and problems due to bad Karma and attain wealth and prosperity.
Milk Abishekam (Hydration Ceremony) to Muruga
Milk is a nourishing ingredient used widely in Abishekam to invoke blessings of good health and long life. Performing Milk Abishekam to Muruga can gain his grace for a long, healthy, and disease-free life.
Honey Abishekam (Hydration Ceremony) to Muruga
Honey is a sweet nectar that is well known for its medicinal properties. Performing Honey Abishekam to Muruga can invoke his blessings for excellence in music and arts, improved intellect, and promote good health and overall wellbeing.
Tender Coconut Water Abishekam (Hydration Ceremony) to Muruga
As per traditional belief, Tender Coconut Water is known to have a healing effect. This Abishekam performed to Muruga can bestow happiness and fulfillment in life while removing impediments to progress and help enjoy worldly pleasures.
Curd Abishekam (Hydration Ceremony) to Muruga
According to Sacred texts, Curd has been used as an ingredient for Abishekam to help bestow family blessings. Performing this Abishekam to Muruga can remove fertility-related problems and help gain his blessing for good and obedient children.
Rose Water Abishekam (Hydration Ceremony) to Muruga
Muruga is a warrior and an epitome of courage, fearlessness, and intelligence. Performing Abishekam to Muruga with Rose Water can help ease your mind of stress and balance your emotions. It can wipe out all sorrow and fears and help you face life boldly.
Turmeric Abishekam (Hydration Ceremony) to Muruga
As per traditional belief, performing this Abishekam to Muruga can gain his favor for sound health and vitality, remove ailments, and bestow auspiciousness and harmonious relationships.
Sandal Paste Abishekam (Hydration Ceremony) to Muruga
This fragrant paste is an expensive ingredient and is widely known for its curative properties. Performing this Abishekam to Muruga can remove any hindrance to accumulating and enjoying wealth and prosperity.
Muruga Homa (Homa For Relief From Debts & Diseases)

Muruga Homa (Homa For Relief From Debts & Diseases)
This Homa invokes the blessings of the warrior God Muruga for relief from debts, diseases, and negativity. His mother, Goddess Parvati, gifted him the Vel (spear), a psychic weapon which is filled with the powers of the Goddess to instill fear and panic in the hearts of demonic forces. Muruga is associated with wisdom, beauty and courage. As per sacred texts, performing Muruga Homa can help overcome enmity and succeed in litigation and other legal issues. This Homa can bestow lasting peace, prosperity, wealth, and instill energy to achieve goals easily.
Muruga is regarded as the Divine warrior, whose blessings can eliminate obstacles and fulfill desires. The grace of this warrior God can destroy demonic forces, resolve real estate problems, confer health blessings and help overcome debts. You can seek the blessings of Muruga and benefit if you are involved in real estate, including the buying and selling of property or moving to a new home or office. His divine blessing can help you overcome negative influences and subdue mental disturbances, resolve litigation issues and provide all-around success if you are involved in the healing arts. Propitiating Muruga by performing the Homa can help you witness positive changes in life and improve your fortune. Connecting with Muruga can also give success in all ventures and improve career prospects.
Muruga Homa is performed by fully qualified Vedic specialists familiar with the nuances of the ceremony and adhering to strict Vedic traditions to ensure the best results. The ritual can be performed during specific Horas. Muruga rules the planet Mars, and Mars rules Tuesday. Hence, it is advisable to perform the Homa honoring Muruga on that day. It can even be performed on the 6th Moon day (Shasti), a power day for Muruga and Mars. Our astrologers ensure the ceremony is scheduled on auspicious occasions and timings depending on the needs of the individual.
According to Sacred texts, offering your prayers to Muruga through this Homa can bestow the following blessings:
Protection from negativity, enemies, and competitors
Improve healing and reinvigorate yourself
Success in real estate dealings and litigation
Simple Muruga Mantra You May Chant or Write Regularly

--- PAGE: Amavasya & Tithi Dosha Remedies | AstroVed.com (https://www.astroved.com/dosha-pariharam/amavasya-dosha-remedies) ---

Amavasya & Tithi Dosha Remedies | AstroVed.com
AMAVASYA DOSHA (SUN-MOON CONJUNCTION CREATES AFFLICTION)
Unfavorable Conjunction of the King & Queen Planets Disturbs Physical & Mental Health
Vedic Remedies to Mitigate Affliction and Bring Stability
When there is a conjunction of the Sun and the Moon in any sign in the birth chart, it creates Amavasya Dosha. The effects of the Dosha are strong that the Moon loses its strength and positivity under the influence of the Sun and remains weak. Having this Dosha (Affliction) in the birth chart can create many physical and mental struggles for the native.
How Cosmic Alignment Creates Amavasya Dosha
The close conjunction of the Moon, the signifier of mind and emotions, and the Sun, which represents the soul coming together in any zodiac sign, creates Amavasya Dosha, which is powerful enough to trigger physiological and mental disturbances.
Vedic astrology calculates a lunar day when the Moon transits 12 degrees from the Sun. The intensity of Amavasya Dosha varies depending on the degree of the conjunction of the Sun and the Moon. Since Rahu rules the Amavasya Thithi (Moon Phase), having Amavasya Dosha in the birth chart is considered inauspicious.
The Amavasya Dosha can create problems and impediments for a native with this Dosha in their birth chart. It can adversely impact
Good fortune enjoyed by the native and family
How to Reduce the Impact of Amavasya Dosha
Perform Tarpanam for ancestors on all Amavasya days.
Do not disrespect your elders, especially your parents.
Donate food items like rice, jaggery, and milk to the poor and needy on Amavasya days.
Worship Goddess Kali as she is the primary deity who can help mitigate the impact of Amavasya Dosha.
’ 108 times on Mondays since Shiva helped the Moon escape his curse.
Consume vegetarian food on Amavasya days.
Homa Recommended to Remedy Amavasya Dosha
Chandi homa invokes the blessings of Goddess Chandi, the very embodiment of the primordial energy of creation, sustenance, and destruction of the entire universe. This homa can neutralize forces that are inimical to your interests, help overcome obstacles, ward off evil eyes, remove curses and cleanse your aura of negativity.
Rudra Homa- This Homa is targeted to pacify and propitiate the nine planets, in order to increase their positive influence and neutralize the negative influence, and also to bring you spiritual empowerment and worldly pleasures.
You may think, dream, imagine, and hope to be a thousand things, but the Sun is what you are! To be your best self in terms of your Sun, cause your energies to work along the path in which they will have maximum help from the planetary vibrations.
This Moon homa (Homa) invokes the blessings of Moon. The Moon rules your mind and emotions and brings success. Regarded as the Queen of Planets, Moon can deeply impact your decision-making skills positively. Performing Moon homa can balance your emotions, improve self-worth, promote love & success and improve rapport with your mother.
At times we face unexpected twists and turns in our lives. They may have the capacity to affect us adversely and turn our lives into disarray. Our lives are closely associated with the cosmic bodies, the planets that play a primary role in shaping our lives. Apart from the date, time, and place of birth, the planets’ positions at the time of our birth in our natal chart signify how our life pans out.
In Vedic Astrology, a Dosha occurs when unfavorable conditions surface in a birth chart. They may be malefic planetary combinations in any one of the twelve houses in the chart, nullifying any positive aspect in the horoscope. A native can feel the adverse impact or effects of the Dosha in the birth chart.

--- PAGE: Chandal Dosh | Remedies for Chandal Dosha | AstroVed.com (https://www.astroved.com/dosha-pariharam/chandal-dosh-remedies) ---

Chandal Dosh | Remedies for Chandal Dosha | AstroVed.com
Unfavorable Placement of Jupiter Disrupting Life
Vedic Remedies to Mitigate Affliction and Generate Positivity
In the birth chart, Chandal Dosha occurs when the planet Jupiter is in conjunction with the malefic planets Rahu or Ketu. Jupiter is called Guru in Vedic Astrology. Chandal refers to an Asura, which is Rahu. Hence, the Dosha is also known as Guru Chandal Dosha. The Dosha can cause malefic effects in the native’s life.
How Cosmic Alignment Creates Chandal Dosha
The Chandal Dosha occurs when there is Jupiter-Rahu or Jupiter-Ketu combination in the birth chart. Jupiter plays a significant role in this Dosha. This planetary placement can make life very difficult for the native.
The combination of Jupiter-Rahu or Jupiter-Ketu in the 3
house can cause Chandal Dosha and give a pessimistic speech. It can also make the native a betrayer and a spreader of hatred.
The Jupiter-Rahu or Jupiter-Ketu combination in the 8
house can create a destructive attitude and may end the life of the native. There may be accidents. If Jupiter and Rahu combine in the 8
house, there may be injuries and pains in the abdominal area, scarring for life.
The combination is lethal if it is in the 9
house, with the native unaware of roots and unlawful. The native can breaks all norms and religious beliefs. They may spread wrong ideas and beliefs and adopt the wrong approach.
However, this Dosha is not very malefic in all houses, and if Jupiter is stronger and well placed, and can control Rahu, then the native could go a long way with minimal loss. Jupiter combined with Ketu is not considered Chandal Dosha by astrologers in the Northern part of India. It makes the native more spiritual and isolated from the world.
How Chandal Dosha Can Impact an Individual
When Chandal Dosha comes into prominence, it can turn the benefic effects of Jupiter into malefic effects. A native influenced by the Chandal Dosha can experience many inconsistencies.
Experience changes in behavior and tendencies
Become disrespectful and inconsiderate to elders
Wear yellow color clothes on Thursdays and frequently, if possible
Consume ghee, turmeric, and yellow Toor dal on Thursdays, if health permits
Donate sweets at a Powerspot to devotees coming to visit them on Thursdays
Do not disrespect your religion or sacred scriptures
Do not disrespect any Guru, Mentor, Teacher, Guide, Yogi, Parents, or Elders
Refrain from doing immoral deeds and act righteously
Follow any Guru/ worship any God you like, meditate upon them and surrender your mind to them
Invoking the blessings of Jupiter can empower you with knowledge, imbibe a positive attitude and bring success in your endeavors.
Invoking Dakshinamurthy, the overlord of the planet Jupiter can help control the malefic effects and boost the positives of Jupiter.
Much of our life has been shaped and influenced by the time of birth. The planetary positions at the time of our birth play a crucial role in determining, with other details, how our life will pan out. Derived from Sanskrit, Dosha signifies physical disorders. In Vedic Astrology, Dosha signifies bad conditions found in the birth chart or horoscope. The planets that occupy the twelve houses in the natal chart will not be placed in a favorable position.
The malefic planets combine to override all good and positive effects in a native’s horoscope, thus causing an adverse impact on their life.

--- PAGE: Remedies for Eka Nakshatra Dosha | AstroVed.com (https://www.astroved.com/dosha-pariharam/eka-nakshatra-dosha-remedies) ---

Remedies for Eka Nakshatra Dosha | AstroVed.com
Affliction Caused By the Birth of Family Members in Same Star
Vedic Remedies to Mitigate Affliction and Strengthen Physical and Material Wellbeing
Eka Nakshatra Dosha occurs when two or more family members are born in the same Nakshatra, next to each other in succession, with no birth happening in between. For instance, a son born in the father’s Nakshatra can cause Eka Nakshatra Dosha. This Dosha, when it occurs in a family, harms the younger and disturbs their health and wellbeing.
How Cosmic Alignment Creates Eka Nakshatra Dosha
When births are happening in a family in the same Nakshatra (birth star), one after the other, Eka Nakshatra Dosha is created. This Dosha adversely impacts the younger born. If both members have the same Nakshatra Pada (every Nakshatra has 4 Padas- 3 degrees and 20 minutes each), the young one may suffer ill-health. If both share only the Nakshatra (birth star) and have different Pada, the younger member might suffer from loss of wealth and unhappiness.
The timing of the suffering starts when the two natives are physically away from each other. Distance causes problems for the younger born. If together, the suffering comes in the form of emotionally and mentally distancing each other.
The younger family member is bound to face adverse effects because of this Dosha.
The younger member starts experiencing physical and mental, or emotional suffering when distanced from the older member
Health issues and other ailments can plague the afflicted member
Disturbances in the form of financial problems and losses may trouble them
Parents unhappy with the afflicted member
Loss of peace and harmony among family members
Chant the mantras of the particular Nakshatra in which the two family members are born.
For instance, if born in the Nakshatra ruled by Jupiter, chant ‘
’ 108 times daily. If not, at least on the day ruled by Jupiter (Thursday)
’ 108 times a day and especially on the day of the planet (Tuesday)
Also, some temples conduct a special Pooja where the parents donate the child (younger sibling) to God. This Pooja makes one sibling spiritually submitted to God, reducing the impact of the Dosha on the family members.
Homa Recommended to Remedy Eka Nakshatra Dosha
This Homa pacifies and invokes the blessings of the nine planets for happiness, harmony, and physical and mental wellbeing.
This Homa invokes the supreme form of Ganesha, Maha Ganapati, to remove obstacles to leading a fulfilling life.
This Homa pacifies and invokes the birth stars (Nakshatras) to reduce the impact of birth star affliction and help enjoy material wellness.
Our life and identity are reflected by the date, time, and place of birth. Most importantly, the position of planets in the birth chart or natal chart (horoscope) can determine our life’s characteristics and different aspects. Planets are carriers of cosmic energies and reflect our past life Karma. They influence and hugely impact our lives.
Derived from Sanskrit, Dosha denotes physical disorders. However, in Vedic Astrology, a Dosha is a bad condition in a native’s birth chart. It occurs when malefic planets occupy a specific position in any of the twelve houses of a native’s birth chart, to the extent that they can even overcome the good and positive aspects that could come to fruition in a horoscope. The presence of Dosha in a birth chart can adversely impact a native’s life.

--- PAGE: Gandmool Dosha | Remedies for Gandmool Dosha | AstroVed.com (https://www.astroved.com/dosha-pariharam/gandmool-dosh-remedies) ---

Gandmool Dosha | Remedies for Gandmool Dosha | AstroVed.com
GAND MOOL DOSHA (AFFLICTION IMPACTING MERCURY AND KETU STAR BORN)
Unfavorable Moon-Star Combination Disturbs Harmony and Wealth
Vedic Remedies to Mitigate Affliction and Bring Stability
Gand Mool Dosha occurs when the Moon is present in any of the six Gand Mool Nakshatras at the time of birth. The Dosha is formed in a horoscope when the Moon is placed in the following constellations: Ashwini, Ashlesha, Magha, Jyeshta, Moola, or Revati, collectively called the Gand Mool Nakshatras. In Vedic Astrology, the Gand Mool Nakshatras are considered inauspicious, and this affliction becomes strong when other vital planets in the birth chart also become malefic.
How Cosmic Alignment Creates Gand Mool Dosha
The Gand Mool Nakshatras Ashwini, Ashlesha, Magha, Jyeshta, Moola, and Revati are ruled by the planets Mercury and Ketu.
The Moon takes around one day to transit a Nakshatra. As the Moon transits from one Nakshatra to the other, at one point, the Moon will be partly placed in one Nakshatra and partly in the other. Gand Mool Dosha will be present in the natal chart if the Moon is placed in any of the four Padas (Divisions) of these Nakshatras at the time of birth.
A native-born in the Gand Mool Nakshatra can face frequent problems in life. Their parents, siblings, and relatives may as well be affected due to this affliction. Such natives may create issues in the family and face obstacles during important endeavors in life. Having this Dosha (Affliction) in the birth chart can cause
Danger to relatives in both father’s and mother’s families
Endangerment to domestic animals and cattle
Dissatisfaction and troubles in the family
How to Reduce the Impact of Gand Mool Dosha
Chant the mantra of Mercury and Moon if the Moon is in Mercury-ruled stars- Ashlesha, Jyeshta, and Revati. The Moon’s mantra ‘
’ is to be chanted 108 times, and the mantra of Mercury ‘Om Sri Budhyaaya Namaha’ or ‘Om Bum Budhaaya Namaha’ is to be chanted 108 times.
Chant the mantra of Ketu and Moon in case the Moon is in Ketu-ruled stars- Ashwini, Magha, and Moola.
Wear green-colored clothes on Mondays and Wednesdays if the Moon is in Mercury Star.
Wear brown or grey-colored clothes on Mondays and Wednesdays if the Moon is in Ketu Star.
Consume spinach more often, and feed spinach and grass to cows in a Goshala (Cow Shelter) on Mondays if possible.
Refrain from seeking pleasures and focus on Wednesdays and Mondays’ spiritual pursuits if the Moon is in Ketu Star.
’ 108 times daily if the Moon is in Gand Moola Nakshatras.
Homa Recommended to Remedy Gand Mool Dosha
Budha Soma Homa (Mercury-Moon Blessings Homa)
Budha, the planet Mercury is an auspicious planet. It is Buddhi Karaka, the significator of intellect and can indicate scholarship, communication skills, and eloquence. It also stands for mind power, career path, and also striving for excellence.
Ketu Soma Homa (Ketu-Moon Blessings Homa)
Ketu is a shadow planet that doesn’t have a physical existence. Being a nodal planet, it functions more as a representative of the overlord of the sign of its placement and can provide results accordingly. Ketu is regarded as Gnyana Karaka, the significator of divine wisdom, and can lead a person towards spirituality.
Maha Ganapati Homa (Supreme Ganesha Blessings Homa)
Ganesha is regarded as the ‘God of new beginnings,’ and almost all new endeavors are commenced with the Ganapati Homa to seek his blessings. By chanting the Ganesha Atharvashirsha during the Maha Ganapati Homa, you can gain the grace and blessings of Ganesha. Whenever you start a new venture or work, this homa can be conducted to remove all blocks and grant success.
Vishnu is the preserver and protector of the universe and one among the Trinity. Performing a sacred homa to Vishnu can bestow you with wealth, good health, happiness, and prosperity.
Rudra Homa- This Homa is targeted to pacify and propitiate the nine planets, in order to increase their positive influence and neutralize the negative influence, and also to bring you spiritual empowerment and worldly pleasures.
Ayush Homa (Longevity and Wellbeing Homa)
Ayush Homa is performed to invoke the blessings of the archetype Ayur Devata, who can bestow you with longevity, good health, and grant relief from mental trauma. The ritual is recommended for those who are suffering from severe health ailments.
Our lives are influenced by the placement of celestial bodies at birth and their transit movement. Their position at the date and time of our birth plays a vital role besides their transit placement. Planets carry cosmic energy, and they are reflective of karmic bondage too.
Dosha occurs when a native has some unfavorable planetary placement or association on their birth chart (horoscope). According to Vedic Astrology, the positioning of the malefic planets in any of the twelve houses in the natal chart can override or overcome the good positives the horoscope may have. This tends to harm the native’s life and not allow positive aspects to bear fruit.

Ghat Dosha Remedies | Remedies for Ghat Dosha | AstroVed.com
GHAT DOSHA (SATURN-MARS CONJUNCTION CREATES AFFLICTION)
Unfavorable Conjunction Disturbs Positivity and Progress
Vedic Remedies to Mitigate Affliction and Bring Happiness
Ghat Dosha occurs when there is Saturn-Mars conjunction in a horoscope. Both are malefic planets and can combine to create havoc in the native’s life. The two most extreme energies of Saturn and Mars can make the native impulsive and develop feelings of hatred, bringing about bad decisions or outbursts with a long-lasting impact.
In the horoscope, Ghat Dosha is formed when Mars and Saturn are placed together in the same sign. This Ghat Dosha can create many malefic effects for a native since the impact of the two malefic planets may be forceful. The native may feel a sense of conflict in all actions due to the restrictions from Saturn. Mars might help to start projects, but Saturn may stall the projects due to being hasty and not planning properly.
Ghat Dosha can create a lot of struggles in the native’s life.
The native may be surrounded and influenced by negative energies
One may face constant struggle and failure in one’s career
There may be a conflict with family members that can create unhappiness
Native may be afflicted with health concerns and diseases
Worship Hanuman on Tuesdays to reduce the ill effects of Mars and Saturn. Chant Hanuman Chalisa on Tuesdays and Saturdays and other days if possible.
Do a 45-day Pooja to Hanuman by keeping red vermillion (Kum-Kum) on his tail for 45 days and seeking his blessings to mitigate the impact of the Ghat Dosha in your birth chart.
Feed the crows on Saturdays and donate food to the poor and needy. Feed stray animals as this also please planet Saturn.
Volunteer in a nursing home on Saturdays.
Give away an iron vessel and blue sari to an older woman on a Saturday.
Light lamps with sesame oil on Saturdays at any Hanuman temple and pray to Hanuman.
Panchamukha Hanuman Homa (5-Faced Hanuman Blessings Homa)
The Panchamukhi Hanuman Homa invokes the amazing five-faced Panchamukhi form of the mighty God Hanuman. Hanuman, also known as Anjaneya, is a staunch Rama Bhakta and is also believed to be an aspect of Shiva. He remains a rare combination of great physical and mental strength, courage and humility, intellect, scholarship, eloquence, and devotion. This monkey-faced God is the divine son of Vayu, the wind God, and remains a Chiranjeevi, an immortal.
Rudra Homa- This Homa is targeted to pacify and propitiate the nine planets, in order to increase their positive influence and neutralize the negative influence, and also to bring you spiritual empowerment and worldly pleasures.
Life is about the planets and stars that align our journey, confirmed at the time of our birth when the planets are in a specific position in our birth chart. Doshas occur in a horoscope when malefic planets like Mars, Saturn, Rahu, and Ketu are in particular houses in the birth chart. This combination of malefic planets will not allow positive aspects of a horoscope to happen. According to Vedic Astrology, the Dosha confirms the malefic positioning of planets or the bad combination of planets in the natal chart, which could cause upheavals in the native’s life.

Grahan Dosh | Remedies for Grahan Dosha | AstroVed.com
GRAHANA DOSHA (ECLIPSE AFFLICTION CAUSED BY RAHU & KETU)
Unfavorable Conjunction of the Sun or Moon with Ketu or Rahu Disrupting Progress
Vedic Remedies to Mitigate Affliction and Strengthen Self
In Vedic Astrology, the Grahana Dosha is the most feared Dosha, formed by Rahu and Ketu. A native would have Grahana Dosha if he or she was born during a solar or lunar eclipse.
How Cosmic Alignment Creates Grahana Dosha
Grahan is Sanskrit for the term eclipse. When Rahu or Ketu, shadow planets known as Chaya Grahas, are in close conjunction with the Sun or Moon at the time of your birth, or if you were born during an eclipse, you incur Grahana Dosha. Grahana Dosha will appear in the birth chart of a newborn when in the birth chart of the native (during the Lagna period), the Sun or Moon is in the same house as Rahu or Ketu.
Possible Cosmic Combination that Creates this Dosha:
Poorna Surya Grahana Dosha- when the Sun and Rahu share a sign
Poorna Chandra Grahana Dosha- when the Moon and Rahu share a sign
Partial Surya Grahana Dosha- when the Sun and Ketu share a sign
Partial Chandra Grahana Dosha- when the Moon and Ketu share a sign
According to Vedic astrology, when a native is born with Grahana Dosha in the birth chart, they may experience the following adverse effects.
Conflicts in marriage and unstable relationships
Inconsistent career with no growth opportunities
Pressure at the workplace and no peace at home
How to Reduce the Impact of Grahana Dosha
Home Remedies for Surya Grahana Dosha (Solar Eclipse Affliction)
Chant Gayatri Mantra 108 times daily at sunrise, especially on Sundays. Offer Argya (water oblation from a copper vessel) to the Sun daily in the morning while chanting the Gayatri mantra.
Donate jaggery to priestly people for seven consecutive Sundays during a good time (Muhurta).
Consult an astrologer and see if you can wear Ruby or keep Surya Yantra at home
Pray to Vishnu daily by chanting his mantras since Sun is said to be an aspect of Vishnu, and his avatars come from the Sun’s lineage.
If you have Surya Grahana Dosha, drinking water from a copper vessel is recommended.
Home Remedies for Chandra Grahana Dosha (Lunar Eclipse Affliction)
’ 108 times a day, especially on Mondays.
Donate milk for four consecutive Mondays during a good time (Muhurta).
Consult an astrologer and see if you can wear Pearl or if you can keep a Chandra Yantra at home.
Pray to Shiva daily by chanting his mantras since Shiva is said to have rescued the Moon from a curse.
You can also drink milk from a silver cup mixed with honey and turmeric, especially on Purnima (Full Moon) night.
Homa Recommended to Remedy Surya Grahana Dosha
You may think, dream, imagine, and hope to be a thousand things, but the Sun is what you are! To be your best self in terms of your Sun, cause your energies to work along the path in which they will have maximum help from the planetary vibrations.
Vishnu is the preserver and protector of the universe and one among the Trinity. Performing a sacred homa to Vishnu can bestow you with wealth, good health, happiness, and prosperity.
Homa Recommended to Remedy Surya Grahana Dosha
This Moon homa (Homa) invokes the blessings of Moon. The Moon rules your mind and emotions and brings success. Regarded as the Queen of Planets, Moon can deeply impact your decision-making skills positively. Performing Moon homa can balance your emotions, improve self-worth, promote love & success and improve rapport with your mother.
Shiva is the archetype of Universal Consciousness, the primordial energy responsible for all possibilities. This homa invokes his blessings to dissolve your karma and bestow health, happiness, and success.
The planets influence our lives by their positions in our natal chart at the time of our birth. Later, they continue to impact our lives when they are in transition. Derived from Sanskrit, Dosha indicates physical disorders. In Vedic Astrology, Doshas occur because of the planets’ unfavorable position in the twelve houses in the birth chart. Malefic planets Mars, Saturn, Rahu, and Ketu, tend to affect the horoscope’s good parts and create Doshas when placed in specific houses. Mars creates maximum Doshas.

Kalathra Dosha Remedies After Marriage | AstroVed.com
Unfavorable Planetary Placement Affecting Relationships
Vedic Remedies to Mitigate Delayed and Troubled Marriages
Kalathra Dosha is an affliction that affects marriage and brings troubles to marriage. In Vedic astrology, the 7
house represents relationships; in Sanskrit, it is called Kalathra Sthana, the house of Spouse. The 7
house signifies spouse, harmony, partnership, happiness, marital harmony, and good bonding. This Kalathra Dosha is also called Vivah Dosha- the affliction affecting marriages.
How Cosmic Alignment Creates Kalathra Dosha
In Vedic astrology, the term Kalathra means spouse. The planet representing spouse is different in male and female horoscopes. Venus represents spouse in a male’s horoscope, and Mars represents the same in a female’s horoscope.
The placement of malefic planets Mars, Saturn, Sun, Rahu, and Ketu in the 1
house from the Ascendant (Lagna) signifies Kalathra Dosha.
The combination of Venus and the malefic planet Ketu in the 7
house can also cause this Dosha, or if Ketu alone is present in the 7
house from the Ascendant or the Moon can also result in Kalathra Dosha.
houses are vital as they help determine the Kalathra Dosha. If a malefic planet is in the 7
house or if there is an association of a malefic planet with the 7
house ruler, it could lead to marital discord. Marriages might be delayed if there is no connection between the 2
Kalathra Dosha can create troubled marriages and disturb peace and harmony. Having this Dosha (Affliction) in the birth chart can bring hurdles and the following issues.
How to Reduce the Impact of Kalathra Dosha
The traditional practice of performing marriage rituals of the native with Kalathra Dosha and a banana tree or Kumbh (Sacred Pot/Kalash). The second option is called Kumbh Vivah
Sponsoring the wedding of a poor couple who wants to get married but doesn’t have money for the wedding.
Visiting and offering prayers at Kalahasti Powerspot in Andhra Pradesh, if possible.
Taking the blessings of an elderly couple other than your parents and donating fruits, grains, clothes, money, and gold, if possible.
Praying to Vishnu and Shiva to remove the adverse effects of this affliction in your chart.
Performing Anna Dhaanam (Food Feeding) at any Powerspot on a Friday.
Offering white lotus flower to Shiva for seven consecutive Fridays.
Distributing sweets at a Powerspot for seven consecutive Fridays.
Homa Recommended to Remedy Kalathra Dosha
This Venus homa (Homa) invokes the blessings of Venus. The planet Venus is ruled by Goddess Lakshmi and governs the female gender. He can empower you to harness your creativity and give expression to your abilities. Perform Venus homa to receive blessings for longevity, wealth, progeny, happiness, property and good education.
This Jupiter homa (Homa) invokes the blessings of Jupiter. Jupiter is the planet of success and teacher of the humankind. He represents the higher mind, the vision of truth, optimism, indulgence, wisdom, and desire. This homa is targeted receive his blessings for success in endeavors, increase imagination, gain knowledge and serve as an inspiration to others.
Mars is fiery and passionate planet. If in your chart, Mars is weak or badly placed he will create debts (credit card, medical, mortgage), diseases and relationship problems. A positive Mars gives courage, healing and financial discrimination. Performing Mars Homa helps control its malefic effects for your well-being.
(Divine Couple Shiva-Shakti Blessings Homa)
Shiva Shakti Homa is a powerful ritual dedicated to the divine union of the universal powers Shiva and Shakti. Shiva is auspiciousness, and Shakti is positive energy in its ultimate form. This homa can be one of the holiest of worships to earn the abundant blessings of these supreme divinities, for much welfare and benefits.
(Kamadeva, the God of Love Blessings Homa)
Kamadeva is the God of love. The term ‘Kama’ means desire, and ‘Deva’ is God. Also known as Manmatha, Kamadeva is referred to in Rig Veda, the most ancient of the Vedas. He is said to be extremely handsome and has the equally beautiful Rathi as his lady love. Kamadeva remains an embodiment of desire and is believed to rule over love that is essentially physical and sensual. He is depicted as holding a sugarcane bow tied to a string of honeybees and uses arrows made of flowers.
Life is never a straight line and has its fair share of ups and downs. Vedic Astrology will tell us that our lives are intertwined with the celestial bodies that have charted our life course. At the time of our birth, the planetary positions in the twelve zodiac houses are considered to draft a birth chart (horoscope). A Dosha occurs when malefic planets are positioned in specific houses in the horoscope. The combination of these malefic planets in the birth chart will not allow the positive Yogas to come to fruition. Thus, a native will experience in their life the adverse impact of Doshas.

Remedies & Effects for Kemadruma Dosha | AstroVed.com
Unfavorable Placement of the Moon Disturbs Life
Vedic Remedies to Mitigate Affliction and Boost Willpower and Abundance
Kemadruma Dosha is one of the most disturbing Doshas (Afflictions). This Dosha holds great significance in a native’s birth chart since it is associated with the Queen planet Moon, the ruler of our mind and emotions. The Dosha occurs when houses before and after the Moon in a natal chart are vacant.
How Cosmic Alignment Creates Kemadruma Dosha
Kemadruma Dosha occurs when there are no planets on either side of the Moon in a birth chart. If the 2
house from the Moon are free of any planets, the inauspicious Kemadruma Dosha occurs.
Native suffering from the Kemadruma Dosha in their birth chart may face numerous struggles and problems in life.
Fast on the day of Purnima for two consecutive years.
Visit a Shiva temple on Mondays and worship Shiva by pouring raw milk on Shiva Lingam. In some practices, people also pour water mixed with sesame seeds.
Chant Sri Suktam (Hymn in Praise of Lakshmi) regularly.
Chant Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra 108 times daily or at least on all Mondays.
Drink milk mixed with honey and turmeric from a silver cup at night and chant ‘
Fast on Mondays, especially on Purnima (Full Moon) and Amavasya (No Moon) days.
Meditate daily and practice Pranayama (breathing techniques).
Donate milk to poor people, mothers, and children.
If possible, consume food from a silver plate.
Homa Recommended to Remedy Kemadruma Dosha
Invoking the blessings of the Moon can help stabilize your emotions and think clearly to make well-informed decisions in life.
Invoking the Goddess of Wealth and the Banker of Heaven in the Homa can help minimize financial losses and scarcity.
Invoking the divine weapon of Vishnu, Sudharshana, in the Fire lab can help improve willpower and build a protective shield against negativity.
The planets, stars, and zodiacs greatly influence our lives and reflect past deeds. At the time of our birth, they play a significant role in determining what we will experience in our life. The planetary placement when we come into this world plays an important role. Their positioning in the natal chart (horoscope) will indicate positive and negative aspects of nature and events that will unfold. The transit of planets, too, will influence our life’s journey.
According to Vedic Astrology, a Dosha occurs when some unfavorable conditions are present in the birth chart. The positions of malefic planets in the twelve houses in the birth chart can overshadow the good aspects. Hence, a native will feel the adverse impact or effects of a Dosha.

--- PAGE: Shani Rahu Shrapit Dosha Remedies | AstroVed.com (https://www.astroved.com/dosha-pariharam/shrapit-dosha-remedies) ---

Shani Rahu Shrapit Dosha Remedies | AstroVed.com
GHAT DOSHA (SATURN-MARS CONJUNCTION CREATES AFFLICTION)
Unfavorable Conjunction Disturbs Positivity and Progress
Vedic Remedies to Mitigate Affliction and Bring Happiness
Shrapit Dosha occurs in a natal chart when Saturn and Rahu are placed in a single sign. Shrapit means a curse by someone from a past life. This Dosha results from the wrong actions of a native in a past life. A native is cursed by someone for their misdeeds in a past life. It is considered bad luck to have past life Karma chasing one’s existence in the present one. Whatever happens in this life is a reflection of bad karma from the past life.
How Cosmic Alignment Creates Shrapit Dosha
There are two ways Shrapit Dosha can happen in a horoscope. It can occur when Saturn and Rahu are placed in the same house, or it could also happen when Saturn can easily aspect Rahu. However, the conjunction of Saturn and Rahu is the main reason for the occurrence of this deadly Dosha.
Having this Dosha in the birth chart means that the person is cursed, and this Dosha can nullify the positive effects of good Yogas (Positive Combinations).
Shrapit Dosha can impact a native badly, reflecting the result of bad karma from the past life. A native can face many hurdles without any visible cause and not know what is affecting them. There are many problems that a native may experience as the malefic effects of this Dosha.
How to Reduce the Impact of Shrapit Dosha
Feed the crows and stray animals on Saturdays.
Feed poor people and help the needy and older people on Saturdays.
Light incense sticks near Hanuman as the smoke is Rahu, and Hanuman can control Rahu.
Chant Rahu and Saturn Mantras 108 times daily.
Light 8 lamps at home with sesame oil facing west on Saturdays.
Keep a horseshoe on the western wall of the house.
Sprinkle Ganges water and rock salt on the western quarter of the house on Saturdays.
Wear gemstone Blue Sapphire for Saturn or Gomed for Rahu only if an astrologer advises.
The Saturn Homa (Homa) is dedicated to the planet Saturn and is performed to appease and harness his blessings to overcome trials and tribulations in life. Saturn’s powerful energy can affect your life and at times may seem to be overbearing, but the purpose of its influence is to guide you towards the right path.
Panchamukha Hanuman Homa (5-Faced Hanuman Blessings Homa)
The Panchamukhi Hanuman Homa invokes the amazing five-faced Panchamukhi form of the mighty God Hanuman. Hanuman, also known as Anjaneya, is a staunch Rama Bhakta and is also believed to be an aspect of Shiva. He remains a rare combination of great physical and mental strength, courage and humility, intellect, scholarship, eloquence, and devotion. This monkey-faced God is the divine son of Vayu, the wind God, and remains a Chiranjeevi, an immortal.
Durga Homa (Negativity Crushing Durga Homa)
Durga Homa- This Homa is targeted to help with dating/marital related issues. Invoke Goddess Durga, who has the power to bless you to overcome marital and relationship issues.
Human lives are closely bonded with the planetary system, their positions, and their movements. Much of what transpires among the celestial bodies they have significantly impacted our lives. From the time of birth, with details of date, time, and place, the planetary positions play a vital role in determining our life.
Planets radiate cosmic energies and are reflective of karmic energies. Derived from Sanskrit (physical disorders), in Vedic Astrology, planets create a Dosha whenever there are malefic combinations or bad placements in a birth chart. It signifies the overriding effects of the malefic planets on the positives in a horoscope that become nullified.

FREE Daily/Today Love Horoscope,Free Daily Romance Horoscope
Love is the key to a successful and happy relationship. While some of us find it lucky to find our perfect partner with minimal efforts, some may find it hard in finding their right partner. There is a term 'soul mate', which denotes your ideal life partner. Finding your soul mate not only requires just physical attraction/compatibility,
financial strength, etc. but also importantly involves the
compatibility of planets in your birth chart with that of your partner.
Love horoscope is that particular branch of Vedic astrology, which helps you in finding out your partner with horoscope compatibility. This involves matching your zodiac sign, birth star and other vital factors with your partner’s horoscope. Love horoscope also answers your long pending love questions such as - when can you propose to your partner, how strong will your relationship shape up in future, when will you get married, etc. and help you build a cordial, loving and happy relationship.

July brings emotional growth, spiritual clarity, and gradual stability for Pisces Moon sign natives. Personal life remains emotionally supportive and comforting. Career matters require patience and careful decision-making, as responsibilities and expectations may increase. Financial conditions improve slowly, helping reduce previous pressures gradually. Professional life remains demanding, with mixed results across different fields. Business activities require practical planning and financial discipline. Health improves gradually through proper rest and balanced routines. Educational activities move positively, bringing stronger concentration and confidence. This month encourages emotional maturity, calm thinking, and balanced communication. Avoid emotional overreactions, unnecessary stress, and impulsive decisions in personal and professional matters. Consistent efforts and practical planning will help Pisces natives maintain stability and steady growth throughout the month.

Nadi Chapters, Advance Nadi Chapter 1, Chapter 13 and Chapter 14.
The Nadi is organized by “kandams” which are specific chapters about your life.
The AstroVed Nadi Essential Package teams Chapter 1, Chapter 13 and Chapter 14.
called the 1st Kandam or General Chapter, indicates year by year the major events for which you are destined.
are remedial chapters that prescribe corrective measures for life transformation.
Once you have completed the Essential Chapters you may wish to opt to have additional chapters read that provide specific information on major areas of your life. Additional remedies are also prescribed in these chapters.
Chapter 1, 13 and 14 are prerequisites before ordering additional chapters.
Choose Additional Chapters ONLY After Having the Essential Nadi Reading Package -Chapters 1,13,14:
Nadi Astrology Chapter 2 – Family and Finance
Chapter 2 describes your current financial status and future predictions in terms of finance potential, family and education. The chapter also highlights your health issues and prescribes measures to lessen your financial burden. By reading this chapter, you redefine your life to lead healthy, wealthy and blissful life with your loved ones.
Nadi Astrology Chapter 3 -Siblings and Courage
Whether you are in search of your sibling or need to understand the compatibility with your siblings, Chapter 3 is the perfect choice for you to apprehend your future with your brother or sister.
Nadi Astrology Chapter 4 – Mother and Comforts
Chapter 4 is ideal for those who are property owners or wish to own a property. It reads vital information about one’s parents, especially that of the Mother whose blessings are indispensable to accumulate wealth and comforts. It contains readings upon your possessions such as buildings/houses, vehicles, land and material pleasures.
Are you longing for a child? Want to know the reason behind your sterility? Chapter 5 explains the reasons for you not having progeny so far and prescribes appropriate remedies. It also highlights information about your children, their birth star and horoscope and few of their future predictions.
Nadi Astrology Chapter 6 – Debts, Disease and Litigation
If you are looking out for the idyllic solution for your financial crisis, chronic illness or legal battle, then Chapter 6 is the shelter for you. This chapter reveals measures to evade from your debts, overcome your illness to live a healthy life and overpower your enemies and win over legal issues.
Find out the right time for your wedding bells (1st Marriage)by reading Chapter 7 Nadi Astrology. This chapter explains the reasons for delay in your marriage and proposes remedial measures. It also reveals when you will be married and for how long will you be married.
Satiate the curiosity to know your life span by reading Chapter 8 which speaks about longevity. The chapter contains valuable information about any mishaps in one’s life, accidents and dangers that one may encounter and the exact details of one’s death such as age, date, time, lagna, location and nakshatra.
Nadi Astrology Chapter 9 – Spiritual Attainment and Fortunes
This chapter helps to know and understand your father better in terms of his wealth, spiritual perception, fortune and philanthropic activities.
Are you finding it difficult to settle down in your dream job? Then this chapter will greatly help you. It gives you information about your profession/career and predictions upon any ups/downs you may face in your professional life and changes in jobs.
Nadi Astrology Chapter 11 – Profits and Second Marriage
If you wish to be an entrepreneur or would like to have an idea about your business profit, this chapter summarizes it all. The chapter also reveals the chances of second marriage in one’s life.
Nadi Astrology Chapter 12 – Expenditure and Losses
Every one of us will be excited to know about our next birth or incarnation. It is a blessing to attain Moksha and liberate self from the continuous cycle of birth and death. This chapter contains information about your future births and also makes you aware of your upcoming expenditures and losses. The chapter is helpful to know about your chances of overseas travel as well.

Dr. Pillai Speaks: Nadi Astrology Not of Human Origin
Nadi Astrology: Science of Thumbprint and Sounds That Sheds Light on Your Soul’s Journey
Also known as Nadi Jyotish, Nadi is the Science of Thumbprints and the Science of Sounds. The great sages of India have etched our destinies on palm leaves as divine predictions. Out of their deep compassion for humanity, these advanced beings wanted to provide an opportunity for people to discover and transform their destinies and evolve their souls. The soul stories that they saw were written down. This mystical science from our ancient seers not only gives predictions to understand the past, present, and future but also remedies to dissolve your karma and lead a successful life.
During ancient times, knowledge was passed generations after generations orally, and the concept of writing or documenting came 3000 years later. Granite slabs, thin copper plates, and tree bark were some mediums used to record information, but palm leaves were the choice for the Nadi scriptures.
The Rishis had notable scribes carefully etch each letter of your story on the dried leaf with a unique tool or stylus. Done all by hand to avoid splitting the leaf, the writing was almost always continuous, without any space between words, which is why Nadi Leaf reading requires training, as well as an understanding of the ancient languages of Tamil or Sanskrit. To preserve these leaves, applying lamp black or turmeric on top of the writing was followed to increase contrast and make it readable. A bundle of leaves in the same thumbprint category, or soul grouping, was then bound with a cord between two wooden covers.
Nadi Astrology uses your thumb impression to discover your Nadi Leaf and predict your destiny from the readings written as a sacred song. That difference in Nadi Astrology sets it apart from traditional astrology, which requires you to give birth details. There are around 108 human thumbprints classifications; from there, your unique thumbprint is detailed to find your soul grouping or a bundle containing anywhere from 20 to 25 individual leaves. Then the Nadi Reader uses the sounds inscribed on the leaves to hone in on your exact leaf.
A great value of the Nadi is that it gives you an understanding of the link from your prior birth to your current life. It also provides the exact ways to transform the residual karma from the previous birth. The Rishis not only wrote down your soul’s roadmap on your Nadi leaf but also prescribed a list of ‘remedies’ or ‘course corrections’ that can reroute you on a promising path.
These remedies often involve visits to energy vortexes to make simple offerings. We are all energetic beings. By resonating with the energy of a specific vortex, you can mitigate the past unfavorable karmic energy signature and get back on the right path. Usually, these energy vortices are temples in India. However, depending on the nature of your karma to be remediated, the Nadi Astrologer prescribes specific Powerspots. The performance of the listed remedies can help negate the residual effects of your past actions and allow you to progress in life.
Nadi reading is a simple 4-step process. Once you place your order for Nadi essential package, your journey starts with the search for your Nadi leaf and ends with the translation of your Nadi leaf. These predictions are uploaded as an MP3 file and remedies as a PDF file against your order in the order log.

Dr. Pillai Speaks: Nadi Astrology Not of Human Origin
Nadi Astrology: Science of Thumbprint and Sounds That Sheds Light on Your Soul’s Journey
Also known as Nadi Jyotish, Nadi is the Science of Thumbprints and the Science of Sounds. The great sages of India have etched our destinies on palm leaves as divine predictions. Out of their deep compassion for humanity, these advanced beings wanted to provide an opportunity for people to discover and transform their destinies and evolve their souls. The soul stories that they saw were written down. This mystical science from our ancient seers not only gives predictions to understand the past, present, and future but also remedies to dissolve your karma and lead a successful life.
During ancient times, knowledge was passed generations after generations orally, and the concept of writing or documenting came 3000 years later. Granite slabs, thin copper plates, and tree bark were some mediums used to record information, but palm leaves were the choice for the Nadi scriptures.
The Rishis had notable scribes carefully etch each letter of your story on the dried leaf with a unique tool or stylus. Done all by hand to avoid splitting the leaf, the writing was almost always continuous, without any space between words, which is why Nadi Leaf reading requires training, as well as an understanding of the ancient languages of Tamil or Sanskrit. To preserve these leaves, applying lamp black or turmeric on top of the writing was followed to increase contrast and make it readable. A bundle of leaves in the same thumbprint category, or soul grouping, was then bound with a cord between two wooden covers.
Nadi Astrology uses your thumb impression to discover your Nadi Leaf and predict your destiny from the readings written as a sacred song. That difference in Nadi Astrology sets it apart from traditional astrology, which requires you to give birth details. There are around 108 human thumbprints classifications; from there, your unique thumbprint is detailed to find your soul grouping or a bundle containing anywhere from 20 to 25 individual leaves. Then the Nadi Reader uses the sounds inscribed on the leaves to hone in on your exact leaf.
A great value of the Nadi is that it gives you an understanding of the link from your prior birth to your current life. It also provides the exact ways to transform the residual karma from the previous birth. The Rishis not only wrote down your soul’s roadmap on your Nadi leaf but also prescribed a list of ‘remedies’ or ‘course corrections’ that can reroute you on a promising path.
These remedies often involve visits to energy vortexes to make simple offerings. We are all energetic beings. By resonating with the energy of a specific vortex, you can mitigate the past unfavorable karmic energy signature and get back on the right path. Usually, these energy vortices are temples in India. However, depending on the nature of your karma to be remediated, the Nadi Astrologer prescribes specific Powerspots. The performance of the listed remedies can help negate the residual effects of your past actions and allow you to progress in life.
Nadi reading is a simple 4-step process. Once you place your order for Nadi essential package, your journey starts with the search for your Nadi leaf and ends with the translation of your Nadi leaf. These predictions are uploaded as an MP3 file and remedies as a PDF file against your order in the order log.
AstroVed works with only those readers of the highest integrity and skill from years of rigorous training. Authentic Nadi Readers are a select few who have learned the art from being passed down from generation to generation in the same clan. Dr. Pillai has done over 20 years of Nadi research and has discovered leaves that are genuine, accurate, and reliable. Our renowned Board of Advisors guarantees that the leaves read by our expert Nadi Readers are from an authentic source. The Nadi readers themselves are specifically chosen and continuously monitored by our Nadi Accreditation.
The Nadi Reading Prediction is different from a Vedic Astrology Prediction. The Nadi reader reads what he sees from the leaves. In general, the Nadi readers are not astrologers and are only trained to read the Ancient Palm Leaves intuitively. If unsatisfied after the Nadi Leaf Identification session, contact

The Science of Sounds- Sounds Divine of Nadi
Certain vital details must be confirmed while identifying your leaf. Of these details, the sound of your name and your parents must match the sounds on the leaf. Your karma follows you through sounds. So this is a foolproof way of identification.
The names are revealed quite miraculously. No matter if you have an African, Western, Japanese, or Chinese name, your name will appear on this ancient leaf. Dr. Baskaran Pillai, AstroVed’s founder, has researched Nadi for over twenty years. He states that “karma,” or the continuation of cause and effect from one birth to another, is done through sounds.
The Nadi can accurately identify your name because it identifies the sounds, in terms of syllables, that create your modern-day identity and karma. The ancient Tamil language does not use many modern-day letters, so some names such as “George” would be identified as “Jo ir ij.”

Navagraha | Navagraha Temple | AstroVed Temple
Nava Graha (the 9 planets) govern every aspect of our life. Their position in the horoscope at the time of birth determines the course of life. Certain unfavorable positions lead to Doshas and cause distress in life. To appease the planets and nullify such Doshas, Navagrahas are worshipped. The nine planets are Surya (The Sun), Chandra (The Moon), Mangal (Mars), Budha (Mercury), Guru (Jupiter), Sukra (Venus), Sani (Saturn), Rahu, and Ketu (Shadow Planets).
: Please remember to choose your service start date after making the payment.
According to Sacred texts, performing Pooja to the Nine Planets can pacify malefic planets and strengthen benefic planets, and grant you a peaceful, harmonious and prosperous life.
Grants strength, health, prosperity, success in all endeavors, and relief from ailments

Navagraha Homa (9 Planets Blessings Homa)
Navagrahas are the celestial bodies and are propitiated as planetary deities in Hindu culture. 'Nava' means nine, and 'Graha' means planets. Vedic Astrology signifies nine planets in our birth chart. They are believed to shape and influence our lives based on their position in our birth chart. Performing the 9 Planets Homa or Navagraha Homa can nullify the adverse effect of afflicted planets, remove obstacles in career and business, help gain wealth and prosperity, reduce physical ailments and maintain stability in life.
According to the scriptures, Navagrahas or the Nine Planets can help overcome suffering or misfortune caused by bad Karma in the past life. They play an important role in aspects of our daily life. Their planetary position and conjunction in our birth chart can control our desires and outcome. According to Vedic texts, performing this Homa can reduce the negative impact of malefic planets and enhance benefic planets’ energy to offset the evil influences.
The Navagraha or the Nine Planets exert their influence on our lives and release Karmic forces that can impact our lives positively or negatively from time to time. Planetary conjunctions in the birth chart can reflect our Karma. As per Sacred texts, the Navagraha or 9 Planets Homa is a powerful ritual that can nullify the impact of Doshas (Afflictions) caused by malefic planets, enable us to overcome adversity with courage, and achieve success and peace. The Homa is performed by fully qualified Vedic specialists familiar with the nuances of the ceremony and adhering to strict Hindu traditions to ensure the best results.
According to Sacred texts, offering your prayers to the Navagraha through this Homa can give you the following benefits:
Nullify Doshas (Afflictions) by malefic planets
You will receive Sacred grey ash from the Homa, which can be kept at your prayer altar. You can duly apply it on your forehead whenever you seek the blessings of the deities.

--- PAGE: Agastya Rishi | Agastya Muni Temple | AstroVed Temple (https://www.astroved.com/temple/agastya-rishi/) ---

Agastya Rishi | Agastya Muni Temple | AstroVed Temple
“Siddhas are more powerful beings. Even Gods and Goddesses don’t displease them. They give you the ability to know everything and do everything.”
Agastya is a legendary Vedic Rishi (Sage) and the head of all Siddhas. Siddhas are people with mystical powers called ‘Ashtamaha Siddhi’ or eight supernatural powers, which they gained through intense meditation. Agastya is also known as one of the Seven Great Sages (Sapta Rishis) and has contributed immensely to the Vedic and Siddha literature. It is believed that these Mantras were revealed to him by the Brahman (Supreme Being) for the welfare of human beings.
: Please remember to choose your service start date after making the payment.
Agastya is the leader of all Siddhas and a great Yogi, who initiated Kriya Yoga with many disciples under him. According to traditional belief, performing Pooja to Sage Agastya can invoke his special powers for healing physical and mental ailments, protect from negativity, and enable you to progress on your spiritual journey towards liberation.

Agastya Homa (Homa for Siddhi Powers and Success)
Agastya is the most revered and prominent among the great sages in Hinduism. He is a celebrated author of several hymns of the Rig Veda. Highly intellectual, he was proficient in many languages. He has often been associated with the term ‘one who brightens’ in darkness. The sage is much revered across the Puranas, both in Shaivism and Vaishnavism. Performing the Agastya Homa can help attain success and mastery over the Siddhi (supernatural) powers.
Agastya was a great and much respected Indian sage featured in ancient relics and sculptures across South and South-East Asia. His images can be viewed in ancient and medieval temples in north and south India. He is also revered as the first and foremost Tamil Siddha. He is called the ‘Head Siddha,’ the Guru (teacher) of all Siddhas. Siddha medicine is the traditional Tamil medicine and Agastya imparted the wisdom of Siddha medicine through verses in Tamil. Performing Homa invoking Agastya with complete devotion can help individuals attain knowledge, intellectual skills, success in endeavors, and Siddhi (supernatural) powers.
Sage Agastya finds prominence in all the four Vedas and is mentioned in the Upanishads and epics of Ramayana and Mahabharata. A highly respected sage in the Vedic era, his ashram was based in Tamil Nadu. Often called the ‘Father of Tamil language,’ he composed the early grammar of the language called Agattiyam. He spearheaded the development of Tampraparniyan medicine and spirituality. Performing Agastya Homa is believed to bring numerous benefits including gaining knowledge, success, and Siddhi powers.

Ganesha and Anjaneya are both perceived as 'Mangal Murtis' and are believed to be extremely auspicious. One half of the deity is Ganesha, and the other half is Anjaneya. While offering worship, it starts with Ganesha, the 'Adi' (beginning), and ends with Anjaneya, who denotes the 'Anth' (end). Anjaneya is an incarnation of Shiva, who is believed to remain immortal even after the creation is destroyed.

Hayagriva | Hayagriva Temple | AstroVed Temple
The name Hayagriva consists of two words, ‘Haya’ meaning ‘horse’ and ‘Griva’ meaning ‘neck.’ This is the horse- necked avatar (incarnation) of Vishnu. Hayagriva is a lesser-known incarnation; nonetheless, he is an important deity in the Vaishnava tradition and is worshipped as the bestower of knowledge and wisdom and the Guru of Goddess Saraswati.
The Hayagriva incarnation of Vishnu appears in Sacred texts such as Mahabharata, Bhagavata Purana, Matsya Purana, Skanda Purana, and Devi Bhagavata. Brahma sought Vishnu’s help to retrieve the Vedas, which were captured by two demons. Vishnu took this form to kill the two demons and return the Vedas to Brahma.
Hayagriva is depicted with a human body and a horse’s head, brilliant white, with white garments and seated on a white lotus. In his four hands, he holds the Sacred disc of Vishnu, holy conch, a book, and the Jnana Mudra (the gesture of knowledge).

Heramba Ganapati – The Protector of The Weak
‘Heramba’ is made up of two words, ‘He’ and ‘Ramba.’ ‘He’ indicates ‘weakness’ or ‘helplessness’; and ‘Ramba’ denotes ‘protection.’ He-Ramba Ganapati is thus the compassionate one who is the ‘protector of the weak and the helpless.’ He is depicted as snow-white in color with five heads and ten hands. His right hand is in Abhaya Mudra (the wish-granting posture) and in his other hands, he holds his own broken tusk, the Japa mala of Rudraksha beads, a garland, his favorite sweet Modak (dumpling), a fruit, a noose, a battle axe, and a hammer. This obstacle-remover protects and grants boons to those who pray to him.

Help you overcome fears, anxiety and doubts

The Sanskrit word ‘Kama’ means desire, and ‘Dhenu’ means giver or provider. Hence, Kamadhenu is the fulfiller of all earnest wishes. According to scriptures, the Divine cow Kamadhenu emerged during the churning of the Milky Ocean (Samudra Manthan), and it houses all the Gods, Goddesses, sages, and Vedas (sacred texts) in it. It is believed that propitiating Kamadhenu invokes the combined blessings of all the Divine beings.
The Homa bestows munificent and multiple blessings of the holy cow Kamadhenu. As a mother cow nourishes her calf, Kamadhenu nurtures and blesses all her devotees with goodness and prosperity. Our trained priests will be performing this Homa with an inclination towards establishing a Divine connection with the deity. This Homa is scheduled at the best possible time to give you the best results. Kamadhenu Homa is a powerful Homa that can invoke the abundant blessings of the wish-fulfilling Cow Goddess for prosperity, wealth, and overall well-being.
According to Sacred texts, offering your prayers to the Divine Cow through this Homa can give you the following benefits:
Grant wealth, prosperity, and material comforts
Provide the combined blessings of all Divine beings
You will receive Sacred grey ash and Kumkum (vermilion) blessed in the Homa, which can be kept in your prayer altar. You can duly apply it to your forehead whenever you seek the Divine blessings of the deities.

Kshipra Ganapati – The Lord of Quick Action
‘Kshipra’ denotes quickness and immediacy. Kshipra Ganapati is regarded as the one who answers prayers, rewards true devotion and satisfies the devotees, immediately. He is depicted as red in complexion with four hands. While the main right arm has his own broken tusk, his other hands hold a sprig of the wish-fulfilling tree Kalpa Vriksha, a noose, and an elephant goad. His trunk curls at its end, and holds a pot of jewels or precious stones known as Ratna Kumbha.

He emerged from the combined cosmic energies of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati as a ball of fire from Shiva’s third eye. He had six faces and was raised by six nymphs called the Krittikas. Thus, the Divine child came to be known as Kartikeya, an amalgamation of beauty, intelligence, courage, and wisdom.

He explained the meaning of the primordial sound, the Pranava Mantra - ‘Om’ to Shiva, stating that the entire creation is contained in ‘Om.’ Hence, he is called Swaminatha Swami, Guru of the Supreme Guru, Shiva. Lord Kuber | Kuberar Temple | AstroVed Temple
Kubera is a Yaksha. Yakshas are the guardians of wealth and Lord Kubera is their king, who is in charge of wealth. He is an important Demi-God and is considered a good friend of Shiva, who also loaned money to Vishnu (Venketeswara) for his wedding. While Goddess Lakshmi remains the Goddess of wealth, Kubera is believed to be its custodian who distributes it among people. Kubera is also the guardian of the Northern direction, who presides over his kingdom Alakapuri. He is the son of Vaisravana and is also believed to be Brahma’s grandson, who originated through his mind. Kubera is depicted as a pot-bellied dwarf with one eye, eight teeth, and three legs. He is fully adorned with jewelry, carries a bag of gold coins and a bowl of pomegranate.

Kubera Homa (Homa to Protect and Improve Financial Wealth)
Kubera is widely revered as the Divine Treasurer of the Gods. The son of Sage Vrishava and Illavida, he is celebrated as the deity of wealth and prosperity. He propitiated Shiva on the banks of the river Narmada and was rewarded by him. Kubera became the Dikpala (guardian) of the North, one of the eight directional deities that guard the universe. Shiva blessed him to be the God of wealth and material comforts. As per Sacred texts, performing the Kubera Homa can alleviate poverty, remove hurdles obstructing growth and success, resolve debts, and enhance wealth and prosperity.
Kubera, also known as the ‘Divine banker,’ safeguards the wealth of the universe. In Sanskrit, Kubera means ‘deformed.’ He is depicted with a short body and a complexion akin to lotus leaves. He has three legs and eight teeth, and his left eye is yellow. He is endowed with a pot of gold coins. He is adorned with jewelry. He acquired the status of a demi-god in the Puranas and other Vedic texts. While Sharad Poornima marks the birthday of Kubera, Dhanteras or Dhantrayodashi remains the most important festival dedicated to Kubera.
Kubera is widely revered as the God of wealth and propitiated along with Goddess Lakshmi, the symbol of wealth and prosperity. He maintains and guards all the treasures of the universe. As per scriptures, performing this Homa can enrich one’s life and help overcome financial distress, reduce poverty, remove hurdles from the path of acquiring growth, stabilize wealth, and bestow prosperity. Trayodasi and Poornima Thithis (13th Moon and Full Moon Phases) are believed to be the most auspicious days to perform the Kubera Homa to invoke his Divine blessings.
As per Sacred texts, performing the Kubera Homa can bestow the following blessings:
Remove hurdles from the path of growth and progress
Reduce poverty and resolve financial distress
Bestow wealth, prosperity, and fulfillment

Durga is the protective mother Goddess, also known as Shakti or Devi. Her name means ‘a fort’ and stands for ‘the one who is invincible.’ She is the embodiment of great power and is one of the Hindu pantheon’s prime female deities. She is also called Durgatinasini, the one who removes sufferings and sorrow. She emerged as a warrior from the Trinity’s creative energy (Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva) to battle the fierce buffalo demon tormenting the world. Armed with celestial weapons gifted by the Gods, she fought the demon and restored peace on earth.

Maha Meru | Maha Meru Temple | AstroVed Temple
Maha Meru is the three-dimensional representation of the Shri Chakra. This is a mystical diagram that consists of nine interlocking triangles with the topmost point called the “Bindu.” At the very top resides Goddess Shakti and her consort Shiva (which symbolizes the union of cause and effect- creation). This cone-shaped structure of the Maha Meru represents the cosmos, divinity, and the human body. The progression of steps leading up to the pinnacle indicates spiritual progression or awakening, ending in enlightenment. The Meru contains the concentrated energy of a million Goddesses in one Sacred form, which transmits energies for prosperity and good fortune.

Maha Vishnu | Vishnu Temple | AstroVed Temple
Vishnu or Maha Vishnu, along with Shiva and Brahma, is part of the Holy Trinity (Trimurti). He is the preserver and protector of the Universe and incarnates on earth to restore balance and Dharma and destroy evil and destructive forces. He is omnipotent and the physical manifestation of spiritual consciousness. The Upanishads and Puranas hail him as the Almighty God who pervades all that is visible and invisible. Vishnu is depicted reclining on Adishehsa (the king of snakes) with his consort Lakshmi, the Goddess of wealth. He has dark blue skin with four hands. The lower two hands have a lotus and mace, and his upper hands have the conch and the powerful weapon, Sudarshana Chakra. Vishnu has a myriad of forms, which he has taken during each Yuga (Epoch) for a particular purpose. His ten avatars are well known as Dashavatara. He guards all living beings on earth and blesses his devotees with longevity, prosperity, peace, happiness, and success.
: Please remember to choose your service start date after making the payment.

Sundaramurthy Nayanar, also known as Sundarar, was a prominent saint of th eighth century. He was also one of the 63 Shaivite saints called Nayanmars, and his parents are also part of the 63 saints. He was called Nambiyarurar at birth, but was affectionately called “Thampiran Thozhan” (Comrade of Lord Shiva) and “Vanthondan” (the argumentative follower). According to local legends associated with Sundarar’s life, he was an attendant of Lord Shiva and went by the name Alala Sundararan. Sundarar composed 3800 hymns, which are contained in the collected works of Thirumurai, called “Thiruppattu.” By rendering these hymns, Sundarar is believed to have performed many miracles. According to Temple lore, the Chola king sought Sundarar’s help to bring rains to his kingdom and remove famine from the land. Sundarar agreed on the condition that 12 acres of land be donated to the Thirupunkoor Temple. He succeeded in his task and obtained 12 more acres as he had to render another hymn to  stop the rains. At another Temple in Avinashi, Sundarar rendered his hymn and resurrected an eight-year-old boy who died after being swallowed by a crocodile. Sundarar is depicted with a hand crutch and lotus flowers in both hands.

The  Nalvar Homa will be performed once every month for 12 months. The Nalvar are four of the foremost fervent devotees of Shiva, the 63 Nayanars. Performing the Nalvar Homa can inculcate sincere faith, and enhance love, devotion, and spiritual growth. It leads to the path of enlightenment to attain the Supreme Being, Shiva.
The most prominent Nayanars (the saintly Shiva worshippers) were Appar, Sambandar, and Sundarar, composers of the Thevaram hymns. The Nayanars emerged between the 7th and 9th centuries. They were extremely pious and were from all walks of life. They dedicated themselves to Shiva worship and composing devotional hymns, sung even today. Along with Manikkavasagar, composer of the Tirukovayar and Tiruvasakam, they are called the Samayacharyas (the teachers of faith). In Tamil, they are called the ‘Nalvar,’ meaning the four. They were the four great Shaiva saints revered across South India. Their songs revitalized the minds of the people and inspired generations down the line.
The Nalvar were proponents of the Shaiva Siddhantha, the philosophy that propagates Shiva is love, which is the key to attaining him, the Supreme Being. The Nalvar provided a resurgence to Shaivism and were believed to be the four pillars, traveling the length and breadth of Tamil Nadu. They kindled the belief that one can live in spiritual bliss to attain supreme liberation, sing and hail Shiva, and the truth of the four holy Vedas. Hence, they were called the Samayak Kuravagal.
The Tamil compilation of the devotional songs by the Nalvar is called Tirumurai. Along with the Vedas, Sacred texts, Shaiva Agamas, and Meykanda Shastras, they form the scriptural basis of the Shiva system of worship in Tamil Nadu. Performing the Nalvar Homa can inspire and instill immense faith and devotion and enhance spiritual growth.
The Nalvar initiated the philosophy of Shaivism- the Shaiva Siddhantha, where a devotee aims to become enlightened through the grace of Shiva. It is a southern tradition and very much in existence. The Nalvar statues are separately installed in some Powerspots in Tamil Nadu. As per traditional belief, performing the Nalvar Homa can instill immense faith, inspiration, and devotion and enhance spiritual growth. It can help one on the path of spiritual elevation.
As per traditional belief, performing the Nalvar Homa can grant the following blessings:
You will receive Sacred grey ash blessed in the Homa. It can be kept in the prayer altar and duly applied on your forehead whenever you seek the blessings of the deities.

Lord Nandi | Nandi Temple | AstroVed Temple
Nandi or Nandi Deva is Lord Shiva’s Sacredbull and serves as his vehicle or mount. He is one of the main Shiva Ganas (the Divine attendants of the Lord) and the gatekeeper of the mountainous abode of Shiva- Kailash.
Nandi is depicted as a white or black bull, adorned with a necklace of bells in a seated position, facing Lord Shiva. He is also represented as half-human and half-bull, wearing beautiful clothes, ornaments, and garlands, with his hands holding a conch, a deer, and a staff, and blessing his devotees. He is often found in front of the Shiva Lingam in most temples and is worshipped along with Lord Shiva.
